name: Atli
description: Atli, also known as Attila the Hun in some interpretations, occupies a complex and often brutal role in Norse sagas. While some scholars debate the direct correlation, the figure of Atli in Norse literature is heavily associated with the historical Attila. In the Volsunga saga and the Poetic Edda, Atli is portrayed as a powerful and ruthless king of the Huns, known for his insatiable greed and treacherous nature. He marries Gudrun, the sister of Gunnar and Hogni, seeking the treasure of the Niflungs (Nibelungs). Atli's desire for the gold leads him to invite Gunnar and Hogni to his hall with malicious intent. He betrays their trust, capturing and ultimately killing them in a brutal manner to seize the treasure. In revenge for her brothers' murder, Gudrun exacts a terrible vengeance upon Atli. She kills their children and then serves their cooked flesh to Atli at a feast before killing him as well, burning down his hall and slaughtering his warriors. Atli represents the dangers of unchecked ambition, greed, and the destructive consequences of betrayal. His story serves as a cautionary tale about the corrupting influence of power and the cyclical nature of violence and revenge within the heroic age.
subtitle: Ruthless King of the Huns, Betrayed
achievements: Instigated the deaths of Gunnar and Hogni
Inspired fear and respect throughout the lands

keyMyths.title: The Invitation to Atli's Hall
keyMyths.description: Atli invites Gunnar and Hogni to his hall, promising them friendship and treasure. However, this is a trap, as he intends to kill them and take the Niflung hoard.

keyMyths.title: The Death of Gunnar and Hogni
keyMyths.description: Atli captures Gunnar and Hogni, demanding the treasure in exchange for their lives. Gunnar is thrown into a snake pit, and Hogni has his heart cut out, yet neither reveals the treasure's location.

keyMyths.title: Gudrun's Revenge
keyMyths.description: Gudrun avenges her brothers by killing her and Atli's children and serving their cooked flesh to him at a feast. She then kills Atli and burns down his hall, slaughtering his warriors.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A Norse saga detailing the history of the Volsung clan, including the story of Atli and Gudrun.
sources.text: A Norse saga detailing the history of the Volsung clan, including the story of Atli and Gudrun.
